title: ranunculus urbicola
intro: Ranunculus urbicola, also known as the lesser celandine, is a perennial plant native to Europe and Western Asia, characterized by its yellow flowers and heart-shaped leaves. The plant has been used in traditional medicine for its anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties, particularly in treating skin conditions such as eczema, acne, and wounds. The health benefits of Ranunculus urbicola include its ability to reduce inflammation, promote wound healing, and exhibit antimicrobial activity. In herbal preparations, the plant is often used in the form of tinctures, infusions, and salves, and is also used in combination with other herbs to create topical creams and ointments.
